results.owner_or_reason: Audit disposition = 'fixed' ŌĆö dev-calls-prod-engine kustomize replacement bug already landed repo-side in commit c2e3a70 (re-verified 2026-07-06), explicitly 'out of wiki write scope'; no delivery-program story references it and none is needed.

breakdown/identity/plan.json E8-S2 (same story as F3) ŌĆö AC1/AC2/AC5 cover the livez(200)/readyz(dependency round-trip)/healthz-not-a-gate split; refs cite 'Audit `YuY9XWpKPS` F3/F4'.
breakdown/identity/plan.json E6-S1 'Two schema-isolated registry stores ŌĆö PII-free routing core + in-jurisdiction sensitive shard' ŌĆö body explicitly cites 'Audit `YuY9XWpKPS` F5'.

breakdown/identity/plan.json E8-S5 'Author A-TOKEN/A-VERIFY + A-SESSION ADRs (SE-Arch ┬¦7 triggers)' ŌĆö body cites 'Audit `YuY9XWpKPS` F6'. (Register itself already landed in arch draft; A-CELL-REGISTRY-as-ADR is explicitly a family-space human action, correctly not a service-space story.)

Stale 'orvex.dev is NOT wired' base-comment fix (httproute.yaml) has no owning story. The only httproute/GitOps-hygiene story in the whole breakdown is breakdown/workflows/plan.json E1-S5 (F-HTTPROUTE), which is scoped to a different audit (`7LGGFR5tGE`, orvex-studio-workflows) and a different repo entirely ŌĆö not this finding.

Knative min-scaleŌēź1-vs-plain-Deployment reconcile + authd/lifecycled binary-name drift: only half-adopted ŌĆö E5.md/E5-S1.md rename the deprovisioner to `cmd/lifecycled` as a Knative Service, but no story anywhere renames the main server binary to `authd` or converts its plain apps/v1 Deployment to a Knative Service. No story cites F8.

Split across breakdown/identity/plan.json E8-S3 (cell-guard/421 + Idempotency-Key, cites 'Audit `YuY9XWpKPS` F9') and E6-S4 (typed tenant-move step-API, cites 'Audit `YuY9XWpKPS` F9'); NetworkPolicy/mTLS lands in E5-S1 and the outbox+relay/CloudEvents envelope lands in E5-S3 ŌĆö all day-1 machinery named in F9 is owned.

config.go's dead CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY field / ignored CLERK_JWKS_URL alignment has no owning story ŌĆö grepped 'CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY'/'CLERK_JWKS_URL' across every breakdown/*/bodies/*.md; the only hit (workflows E1-S5) is a different repo's config drift, not identity's. E1-S2 (the JWKS verifier story) implements verification but never mentions reconciling the config struct's key names.
